rationale: The cited paper is used as a key prior study in the credit-card minimum-payment literature, not just as a background mention. The citing paper explicitly says it replicates the prior finding that showing minimum payment information lowers manual repayments, citing Navarro-Martinez et al. (2011) directly, and then extends that design with new intervention prompts. It also uses the cited study in framing prior evidence on ineffective disclosure nudges and in setting experimental bill/minimum values, indicating meaningful influence on positioning and study design.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Navarro-Martinez et al. (2011) by reproducing its minimum-payment anchoring result in a new experiment and then adding new mitigation interventions (full-repayment and low-payment prompts). It extends that earlier line of work from documenting the effect to testing practical remedies and quantifying broader consequences in field data.
